title: DNA damage assessment in the adult Drosophila brain via pH2Av (S137) immunostaining
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ddvjq","text":"This protocol describes how to stain adult Drosophila brains with pH2Av to assess DNA damage.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Fix flies of the desired genotype Overnight in 4% formalin, embedded heads in paraffin, cut 4-micron sections, and dry at 42˚C Overnight
Deparaffinize and bring through ethanol to water (xylene x 2, 100% ethanol x 2, 95% ethanol x 2, H2O x 2)
Microwave slides in 10 mM sodium citrate for 00:15:00 . Cool for 00:20:00 . Stock: 100 mM sodium citrate, pH 6.0. Use at least 1 L of citrate solution in a large glass box to avoid drying
Block in blocking solution (PBS with 0.3% Triton and 2% dry milk) for 30min to 01:00:00
Incubate with primary antibody (Rockland pH2AV, cat # 600-401-914, dilution 1:100) overnight at room temperature
Wash 3 x in PBST (PBS with 0.3% Triton)
Incubate with fluorophore-coupled secondary antibody (Thermo Fisher) diluted 1:200 in blocking solution at room temperature
Wash 2 x in PBST and 1 x in PBS
Mount in medium containing DAPI (Southern Biotech)
creator: Beatrice Weykopf
